---
title: "AI-ready QC"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{AI-ready QC}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

Spectreasy AI-ready QC is a local deterministic export, not an AI integration.
Spectreasy sends nothing, stores no provider key, and uploads no data. Existing
HTML/PDF reports remain the visual scientific reports; the canonical JSON,
human-readable exports, prompt, report summary, and cockpit view derive from one
`spectreasy_ai_qc` object. Raw event-level matrices are excluded.

```r
qc <- collect_ai_qc(controls = ctrl, samples = unmixed, M = ctrl$M,
                    scope = "combined", privacy = "standard")
bundle <- export_ai_qc(qc, output_dir = "spectreasy_outputs/ai_qc")
prompt <- build_ai_qc_prompt(qc, detail = "standard")
```

The standard prompt includes aggregates and selected evidence; full matrices
remain in JSON/CSV artifacts. Standard privacy aliases samples and removes
absolute paths. Strict also aliases control files and removes operator, date,
project, and free-text metadata. `none` requires explicit selection. No privacy
mode includes raw events.

Reference profiles may be built from reviewed clean runs and selected with
`reference = "auto"`, an explicit profile, or `"none"`. Auto-selection matches
cytometer, detector schema, unmixing method, panel range, and AF configuration.
Fewer than five compatible reviewed clean runs produces descriptive ranges but
does not grade empirical metrics.

AI interpretation is advisory. Verify claimed defects against metric
availability, definition, residual profile, grade provenance, alternative
explanations, and the visual report before changing a panel or acquisition.
